card.intuition: A utility-first CSS framework that scans your source code for class names (like `flex`, `pt-4`) and generates a minimized CSS file containing ONLY the styles you actually used.
card.analogy: A custom toolbelt: instead of buying a giant pre-filled toolbox where you only use 3 tools (Bootstrap), you tell a robot (Tailwind CLI) exactly which 3 tools you need, and it builds you a tiny, perfectly sized belt.
when_to_use: Rapidly prototyping UIs without leaving your HTML/JSX
Ensuring consistent spacing and color tokens across a project
Keeping production CSS bundles as small as possible (usually < 10KB)
gotchas: Tailwind CANNOT see classes created with string concatenation (e.g., `text-${color}-500`). Use full class names so the scanner can find them
It requires a build step (PostCSS or Tailwind CLI) to generate the CSS

exercise.prompt: Identify the Tailwind compiler engine (introduced in v3) that generates CSS on-the-fly as you type.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.upper() == "JIT"
assert check("JIT")
print("PASS")
